user_query: I need an event-driven folder watcher I can run locally to keep me out of trouble. Please implement and run a small automation that watches the folder incoming/ for newly added photo metadata files (*.json). When a new metadata file appears, decide whether the shot is sensitive based on the rules in config/rules.yaml, then write structured results to reports/. Use the sample metadata files in input/queue/ to simulate new arrivals by copying them into incoming/ while your watcher is running. Requirements: 1) You must read and honor config/rules.yaml (do not hardcode rules). A shot is sensitive if any of these fire: its GPS falls within any restricted_zones bounding box; its local time-of-day falls within quiet_hours (the interval may wrap past midnight); or any subject matches do_not_shoot_subjects (exact, case-sensitive match). Otherwise mark it ok. For time-of-day comparisons, use the time portion of captured_at as-is; you do not need to adjust time zones beyond parsing the string. 2) For each processed metadata file, create reports/details/<basename>.decision.json with fields: file_name, status (sensitive|ok|invalid), triggers (array of strings naming matching rules: zone id, quiet_hours, or subject:<name>), captured_at, gps (lat, lon), subjects. If the input JSON is missing required fields, set status=invalid and triggers=["invalid"] but still write the detail file. 3) Append a row to reports/detections.csv with columns: processed_at (ISO datetime you decide), file_name, status, triggers_joined (semicolon-separated), lat, lon. 4) Maintain reports/summary.json with running counts: total_processed, sensitive, ok, invalid. 5) Ignore non-JSON files and process each file at most once. Deliverables: implement the watcher (your choice of language/tools), run it, and simulate events by feeding all files from input/queue/ into incoming/. Leave the resulting reports/detections.csv, reports/summary.json, and reports/details/*.decision.json in place under reports/. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
